topic: Someone discovers their teenager has a secret social media account
raw_output:
[story 1]

I found the login details tucked inside a forgotten textbook. My jaw tightened as I scrolled through the profile. It was a whole other world—filtered sunsets, curated laughter, friends I didn't recognize. I felt a slow, heavy pressure building behind my eyes. When Leo finally walked in, looking for his headphones, I didn't look up. I just pointed at the screen, my voice flat, "What is this?" His face flushed a deep crimson, and he mumbled something about needing privacy, which only made the tightness in my chest intensify.

[story 2]

The notification popped up on my phone, a silent, insistent chime. I picked it up, expecting a spam email, but it was a new profile picture of Maya, one of the girls Leo claimed he didn't talk to. A sharp intake of breath escaped me. I slammed the phone face down onto the desk. I stood up abruptly, pacing the length of the room, my knuckles white where I gripped the edge of the counter. When he finally emerged from his room, looking unusually defensive, I just crossed my arms and stared at him, the silence between us thick and charged.

[story 3]

It was the sheer volume of the posts that got to me—the effortless confidence, the constant stream of validation. I clicked through, my fingers moving mechanically, a strange knot forming in my stomach. I felt a distinct urge to shove the device away. When I confronted him later, my tone was clipped, every word delivered with an edge I didn't intend. "Why are you hiding this?" I asked, my voice dangerously low. He just shrugged, avoiding my gaze, and the tension in the air seemed to solidify into something brittle.
